'''Godherdt''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] who served in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Godherdt was stationed on the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] personal [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']]. Godherdt was an elite [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]] and a key operator of [[magnetic signature sensor]]s monitoring [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Hull/Legends|hulls]]. In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Godherdt was present when Vader arrived aboard the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] to oversee the [[Battle station/Legends|battle station's]] final stages of construction.

==Biography==
[[Captain/Legends|Captain]] Godherdt was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] who served as an elite [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|naval forces]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. He was a key operator of [[magnetic signature sensor]]s monitoring [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Hull/Legends|hulls]].<ref name="CCG1" /> Godherdt was stationed aboard<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|349|Godherdt}}</ref> the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] personal [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]],<ref name="ssd-db">{{DB|starship|superstardestroyer|Super Star Destroyer}}</ref> the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']], as a [[sensor monitor]], keeping data on the integrity of the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer's]] hull.<ref name="CSWE" />

In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] to the beginning of [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].</ref> Godherdt was among the Imperial personnel present in one of the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star's]] [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] bays when [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader arrived aboard the [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] to take direction of its lagging construction schedule. Vader informed the [[Space station/Legends|station's]] [[Commander/Legends|commander]], [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Tiaan Jerjerrod/Legends|Tiaan Jerjerrod]], that [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] was displeased with the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star's]] lack of progress and that he would soon be arriving himself to personally oversee the remainder of the [[Space station/Legends|station's]] construction.<ref name="ROTJ">{{Film|VI}}</ref> At some point around the same [[Time/Legends|time]] period, Godherdt was among the [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officers]] accompanying Vader and Jerjerrod as they strode down a corridor.<ref name="Imperial Command">{{CCG|set=Death Star II Limited|cardname=Imperial Command}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
Godherdt had [[Color/Legends|graying]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and light skin.<ref name="CCG1" />

==Skills and abilities==
Godherdt was technically brilliant<ref name="CCG1" /> and known for being a skilled fleet engineer and [[Sensor officer/Legends|sensor officer]].<ref name="CSWE" />

==Equipment==
Captain Godherdt wore the standard-issue [[Imperial officer's uniform/Legends|Imperial officer's uniform]] with both [[Rank insignia plaque/Legends|rank insignia plaque]] and a [[Code cylinder/Legends|code cylinder]].<ref name="ROTJ" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:MoffJerjerrodGreetsVader.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A scene from the ''Special Edition'' of ''The Empire Strikes Back'', reused from Vader's arrival on the Death Star in ''Return of the Jedi'']]
The opening scene of the [[1983]] film [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] was Captain Godherdt's first appearance, in which he awaited the arrival of Darth Vader on the second Death Star. He was played by an uncredited actor.<ref name="ROTJ" /> The character was given a name and a brief backstory in the [[2000]] ''[[Death Star II Limited]]'' expansion set of the [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]].<ref name="CCG1" />

Godherdt was originally set to appear in another scene in ''Return of the Jedi'', which was ultimately deleted from the film's final version, according to [http://www.starwarz.com/tbone/index.php?categoryid=11&p2_articleid=5 several images] found on [http://www.starwarz.com/tbone/index.php?categoryid=1 T-bone's Star Wars Universe] fan Web site. The first four out of six images in the previous link show Vader and a group of Imperial [[Officer/Legends|officers]] (Godherdt included) heading toward the Death Star's docking bay to greet the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] upon his arrival, an extended version of a scene shown later in the final movie cut.<ref name="T-bone">{{WebCite|url=http://www.starwarz.com/tbone/index.php?categoryid=11&p2_articleid=5|text=T-bone's Star Wars Universe - Return of the Jedi - Off To See The Emperor|author=T-bone|date=2003-06-03|work=T-bone's Star Wars Universe|archivedate=20071112111838}}</ref> A still from that deleted scene was used for the [https://res.starwarsccg.org/cards/Images-HT/starwars/DeathStarII-Dark/large/imperialcommand.gif "Imperial Command" card] for the ''Death Star II Limited'' set, though the card lore never specified if it depicted the same event in-universe.<ref name="Imperial Command" />

The [[1997]] [[The Star Wars Trilogy Special Edition|''Special Edition'']] release of [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']] reuses the original footage from ''Return of the Jedi'' showing Vader's arrival on the second Death Star to depict Vader's arrival aboard the ''Executor'', following his [[Duel on Cloud City/Legends|lightsaber duel]] with [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] on [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]]. As such, the actor who portrayed Godherdt can be seen in ''The Empire Strikes Back'' ''Special Edition'' alongside the other extras who portrayed Imperial personnel on the Death Star.<ref name="ESB">{{Film|V}}</ref> However, the Godherdt character is not [[canon]]ically established to appear in this ''Executor'' scene.
